init.title: Edit SmartShading settings
init.description: Here you can edit the weather, comfort, schedule, presence, and shading settings for this zone, and add, edit, or remove windows.

weather.title: Weather and solar inputs
weather.description: All optional. A weather entity (e.g. DWD, yr.no) provides condition, cloud cover, and wind data automatically. Dedicated sensors override the weather entity for more precise local readings. Solar radiation must come from a dedicated sensor — it is not read from weather entities.

data.wind_speed_sensor_id: Wind speed sensor (m/s) — use wind gust for awnings/screens if available
data.rain_sensor_id: Rain sensor (binary on/off or numeric mm/h rate) — optional
data.ema_alpha: EMA smoothing factor (0.05–1.0) — lower = smoother, higher = more responsive

lifecycle.title: Schedule settings
lifecycle.description: Select how SmartShading detects night and morning. "Earliest trigger" fires as soon as either the configured time or the sun condition is reached — it does not combine or clamp them into a time window.

data_description.schedule_mode: "Same every day" uses one schedule for the whole week. "Weekday / Weekend" lets you set different times and positions for Mon–Fri versus Sat–Sun.

lifecycle_detail.title: Schedule details
lifecycle_detail.description: Enter the time or sun elevation for each trigger. Only the fields relevant to your selected trigger are shown.

data.night_sun_elevation: Custom night elevation (°) — only used when Custom is selected
data.morning_sun_elevation: Custom morning elevation (°) — only used when Custom is selected

data_description.night_position: Cover position during the night phase (0 = closed, 100 = open).
data_description.morning_position: Cover position at the start of the morning phase (0 = closed, 100 = open).
data_description.weekday_night_position: Cover position during the night phase on weekdays (0 = closed, 100 = open).
data_description.weekday_morning_position: Cover position at the start of the morning phase on weekdays (0 = closed, 100 = open).
data_description.weekend_night_position: Cover position during the night phase on weekends (0 = closed, 100 = open).
data_description.weekend_morning_position: Cover position at the start of the morning phase on weekends (0 = closed, 100 = open).
data_description.active_months: Leave empty to apply this schedule in every month. Select specific months to restrict it — for example September through March for a winter schedule.
data_description.night_sun_event: Optional. If set, this astronomical event replaces the night time above. Dawn and dusk use civil twilight (sun at -6°).
data_description.morning_sun_event: Optional. If set, this astronomical event replaces the morning time above. Dawn and dusk use civil twilight (sun at -6°).
data_description.night_not_before: Optional. If the resolved night trigger would occur earlier than this, it is delayed to this time instead.
data_description.night_not_after: Optional. If the resolved night trigger would occur later than this, it is brought forward to this time instead.
data_description.morning_not_before: Optional. If the resolved morning trigger would occur earlier than this, it is delayed to this time instead.
data_description.morning_not_after: Optional. If the resolved morning trigger would occur later than this, it is brought forward to this time instead.

presence.title: Presence settings
presence.description: Optional. If no one is selected, SmartShading always assumes someone is present and never triggers absence-based closing.

data_description.absence_position: Cover position while the zone is unoccupied, unless a stronger protection decision applies (0 = fully closed, 100 = fully open). Applies to all windows in this zone.
data_description.presence_policy: Controls how multiple presence entities are combined into one signal. Anyone home (default): present as soon as one selected person is home. Everyone home: present only when all selected people are home. Inverted: reverses the meaning of "anyone home" — present only once someone is confirmed away.

comfort.title: Comfort settings
comfort.description: Configure shading protection features. Indoor temperature sensors are optional.

data_description.indoor_temperature_sensor_ids: One or more indoor sensors. When multiple sensors are configured, the average of all valid readings is used.
data_description.heat_protection_enabled: Prevents overheating by shading proactively based on sun position, weather, and temperatures.
data_description.glare_protection_enabled: Reduces direct sunlight when the sun hits the window.
data_description.solar_gain_enabled: Keeps shading more open in cool weather to support passive warming. Heat protection has priority.
data_description.glare_min_exposure_wm2: Glare protection only triggers when the window is in the sun's sector and the effective window exposure reaches at least this value. This prevents geometric sun position alone, without meaningful irradiance, from causing unnecessary shading.
data_description.heat_protection_hysteresis_c: How many degrees the temperature must drop below the heat protection threshold before shading is released again. Prevents rapid toggling near the threshold. Set to 0 to release exactly at the threshold (legacy behavior).

behavior.title: Basic shading behavior
behavior.description: Base target cover positions per shading intensity. SmartShading uses these values as starting points and may adapt them per window when enough learning data is available.

add_window.title: Add a window
add_window.description: Add another window to this zone. SmartShading works with a precise azimuth — the compass direction is just a convenience shortcut.

data.floor_level: Floor level (-1 = basement, 0 = ground floor, 1 = 1st floor, …)
data.custom_azimuth: Custom azimuth (0-359°, only used if Orientation is set to Custom)

data_description.absence_position: Leave empty to inherit the zone's default absence position. Set a value to override it for this specific window only.
data_description.window_behavior_mode: Fully automatic: sun, comfort, absence, and schedule active. Absence and schedule: absence control, night, and morning schedule active; no solar, heat, or glare shading. Absence only: absence control only. Automation disabled: no automatic shading. Safety guards (storm/wind) always remain active.
data_description.floor_level: Used to derive the minimum sun elevation for direct exposure. −1 (basement) → 20°, 0 (ground floor) → 15°, 1 (1st floor) → 10°, 2 (2nd floor) → 5°, 3 and above → 0°. For windows in light wells or with heavy obstructions, use obstruction zones for more precise control.

add_window_cover_group.title: Covers for this window
add_window_cover_group.description: Select the cover entity or entities that shade the window "{window_name}". Only add covers that shade exactly this window surface. For separate windows, add separate windows.

add_window_loop.title: Add another window?
add_window_loop.description: {window_count} new window(s) added to this zone. Would you like to add another window?

data.floor_level: Floor level (-1 = basement, 0 = ground floor, 1 = 1st floor, …)
data.custom_azimuth: Custom azimuth (0-359°, only used if Orientation is set to Custom)

data_description.floor_level: Used to derive the minimum sun elevation for direct exposure. −1 (basement) → 20°, 0 (ground floor) → 15°, 1 (1st floor) → 10°, 2 (2nd floor) → 5°, 3 and above → 0°. For windows in light wells or with heavy obstructions, use obstruction zones for more precise control.

data_description.absence_position: Leave empty to inherit the zone's default absence position. Set a value to override it for this window only.
data_description.night_position: Leave empty to use the zone default night position. Set a value to override the night position only for this window.
data_description.window_behavior_mode: Fully automatic: sun, comfort, absence, and schedule active. Absence and schedule: absence control, night, and morning schedule active; no solar, heat, or glare shading. Absence only: absence control only. Automation disabled: no automatic shading. Safety guards (storm/wind) always remain active.
data_description.light_shade_position: Leave empty to use the zone default. Set a value to override the light shade target position for this window only.
data_description.normal_shade_position: Leave empty to use the zone default. Set a value to override the normal shade target position for this window only.
data_description.strong_shade_position: Leave empty to use the zone default. Set a value to override the strong shade target position for this window only.

data_description.manual_sun_sector_enabled: Start and end azimuth are suggested from the window's orientation and tolerance. Enable to use this range instead of the automatic sun sector. The values can be adjusted before saving. Supports wrap-around (e.g. 330° to 30° crosses north).
data_description.obstruction_1_enabled: Enable to mark an azimuth range where a physical obstruction (tree, building, roof overhang) blocks direct sun. Leave elevation fields empty to block at every sun elevation in the azimuth range.
data_description.obstruction_1_block_from_elevation_deg: Optional. Obstruction blocks only when sun elevation is at or above this value. Example: 45° blocks a roof overhang that only shades at steep sun angles. Leave empty for no lower limit.
data_description.obstruction_1_block_until_elevation_deg: Optional. Obstruction blocks only when sun elevation is at or below this value. Example: 18° blocks a mountain or neighbouring building that only affects low sun. Leave empty for no upper limit.
data_description.obstruction_2_enabled: Second optional obstruction zone for this window.
data_description.obstruction_2_block_from_elevation_deg: Optional lower elevation bound for zone 2 (see zone 1 description).
data_description.obstruction_2_block_until_elevation_deg: Optional upper elevation bound for zone 2 (see zone 1 description).
data_description.obstruction_3_enabled: Third optional obstruction zone for this window.
data_description.obstruction_3_block_from_elevation_deg: Optional lower elevation bound for zone 3 (see zone 1 description).
data_description.obstruction_3_block_until_elevation_deg: Optional upper elevation bound for zone 3 (see zone 1 description).

data.night_block_on_window_open: Block night move while window is open (Option A)
data.night_lift_on_window_open: Lift to ventilation position when window opens at night (Option B)
data.window_open_night_position: Ventilation position when window is open at night (%)

data_description.contact_sensor_entity_ids: One or more binary_sensor entities that report the window open/closed state. With several contacts the window counts as open as soon as at least one is open. Leave empty to disable contact-based night logic for this window.
data_description.night_block_on_window_open: Option A: if the window is open when the night drive is due, or the contact state is unclear, the automatic night drive is blocked. SmartShading closes the cover only once the window is safely closed. Requires a window contact.
data_description.night_lift_on_window_open: Option B: if the window is opened at night after the night position has been reached, SmartShading moves to the ventilation position; when the window is closed it returns to the night position. Requires a window contact (not Option A).
data_description.window_open_night_position: Target cover position (HA convention: 0=closed, 100=open) for Option B. Leave empty to use 100% open (fully open for ventilation). Set a lower value to allow ventilation while maintaining some privacy.

remove_window.title: Remove window
remove_window.description: Select the window to remove. This will permanently remove the window and its cover assignments from this zone. Cover entities themselves are not deleted.

remove_window_confirm.title: Confirm removal
remove_window_confirm.description: Remove window "{window_name}"? This cannot be undone.

lifecycle_profiles.title: Lifecycle profiles
lifecycle_profiles.description: Manage named lifecycle schedules and choose which one is currently active.

add_lifecycle_profile.title: Add lifecycle profile
add_lifecycle_profile.description: Create a new named schedule. It behaves exactly like the main Schedule settings, but only applies once selected as the active profile.

data.night_sun_elevation: Custom night elevation (°) — only used when Custom is selected
data.morning_sun_elevation: Custom morning elevation (°) — only used when Custom is selected

data_description.active_months: Leave empty to apply this schedule in every month. Select specific months to restrict it — for example September through March for a winter schedule.
data_description.night_sun_event: Optional. If set, this astronomical event replaces the night time above. Dawn and dusk use civil twilight (sun at -6°).
data_description.morning_sun_event: Optional. If set, this astronomical event replaces the morning time above. Dawn and dusk use civil twilight (sun at -6°).
data_description.night_not_before: Optional. If the resolved night trigger would occur earlier than this, it is delayed to this time instead.
data_description.night_not_after: Optional. If the resolved night trigger would occur later than this, it is brought forward to this time instead.
data_description.morning_not_before: Optional. If the resolved morning trigger would occur earlier than this, it is delayed to this time instead.
data_description.morning_not_after: Optional. If the resolved morning trigger would occur later than this, it is brought forward to this time instead.
data_description.schedule_mode: "Same every day" uses one schedule for the whole week. "Weekday / Weekend" lets you set different times and positions for Mon–Fri versus Sat–Sun.

data.night_sun_elevation: Custom night elevation (°) — only used when Custom is selected
data.morning_sun_elevation: Custom morning elevation (°) — only used when Custom is selected

data_description.active_months: Leave empty to apply this schedule in every month. Select specific months to restrict it — for example September through March for a winter schedule.
data_description.night_sun_event: Optional. If set, this astronomical event replaces the night time above. Dawn and dusk use civil twilight (sun at -6°).
data_description.morning_sun_event: Optional. If set, this astronomical event replaces the morning time above. Dawn and dusk use civil twilight (sun at -6°).
data_description.night_not_before: Optional. If the resolved night trigger would occur earlier than this, it is delayed to this time instead.
data_description.night_not_after: Optional. If the resolved night trigger would occur later than this, it is brought forward to this time instead.
data_description.morning_not_before: Optional. If the resolved morning trigger would occur earlier than this, it is delayed to this time instead.
data_description.morning_not_after: Optional. If the resolved morning trigger would occur later than this, it is brought forward to this time instead.
data_description.schedule_mode: "Same every day" uses one schedule for the whole week. "Weekday / Weekend" lets you set different times and positions for Mon–Fri versus Sat–Sun.

remove_lifecycle_profile.title: Remove lifecycle profile
remove_lifecycle_profile.description: Select the profile to remove. This cannot be undone.

remove_lifecycle_profile_confirm.title: Confirm removal
remove_lifecycle_profile_confirm.description: Remove profile "{profile_name}"? This cannot be undone. If this is the active profile, the schedule falls back to the legacy default.

select_active_lifecycle_profile.title: Select active profile
select_active_lifecycle_profile.description: Choose which lifecycle profile is active. "Legacy default" uses the schedule configured in Schedule settings.

manual_override.title: Manual Override
manual_override.description: Configure how an active Manual Override behaves: how long it lasts, and whether Comfort or Protection actions may still run while it is active.

data_description.override_release_strategy: Controls how the override ends: after a fixed duration, at a fixed time, at the next schedule transition, on the next automatic decision, or only when cleared manually.
data_description.override_fixed_until: Only used when release strategy is set to "Fixed time". If already passed today, the override ends at this time tomorrow.
data_description.override_allow_comfort_actions: When enabled, regular comfort shading may still run while an override is active. The override itself stays active.
data_description.override_allow_protection_actions: When enabled, protection actions (absence, heat, glare) may still run while an override is active. The override itself stays active.
data_description.override_safety_timeout_enabled: Optional maximum duration as a safety net for open-ended strategies, so an override is never permanently forgotten.
data_description.override_duration_min: How long a daytime override lasts before SmartShading resumes normal control (legacy duration mode).
data_description.override_night_duration_min: Safety-net cap for a night-time override. The real release is the morning schedule transition.
data_description.override_detection_tolerance: Minimum position change (in percent) before a manual movement is recognized as an override.

error.cannot_remove_last_window: Cannot remove the last window. A zone must have at least one window.
error.obstruction_elevation_range_invalid: "Blocked from elevation" must not be greater than "Blocked until elevation".
error.night_lift_requires_night_block: Option B (lift to ventilation position) requires Option A (block night move) to be enabled.
error.contact_sensor_required_for_block: Option A requires a contact sensor to be selected.
error.contact_sensor_required_for_lift: Option B requires a contact sensor to be selected.
error.invalid_glare_min_exposure: Enter a glare minimum exposure between 0 and 500 W/m².
error.invalid_heat_hysteresis: Enter a heat protection release margin between 0 and 5 °C.
error.night_clamp_window_invalid: Night earliest trigger must not be later than night latest trigger.
error.morning_clamp_window_invalid: Morning earliest trigger must not be later than morning latest trigger.
error.override_fixed_until_required: Enter a fixed end time when duration is set to "Fixed time".

abort.no_options_for_system_entry: The SmartShading System entry has no configurable options.
abort.no_lifecycle_profiles: No lifecycle profiles have been created yet. Add one first.
abort.lifecycle_profile_not_found: This lifecycle profile could not be found. It may have been removed.

user.title: SmartShading
user.description: Set up SmartShading. You can add windows on the next screens.

weather.title: Weather and solar inputs (optional)
weather.description: All optional. A weather entity (e.g. DWD, yr.no) provides condition, cloud cover, and wind data automatically. Dedicated sensors override it for more precise local readings. Solar radiation requires a dedicated sensor. You can skip this step and add inputs later.

data.wind_speed_sensor_id: Wind speed sensor (m/s) — use wind gust for awnings/screens if available
data.rain_sensor_id: Rain sensor (binary on/off or numeric mm/h rate) — optional
data.ema_alpha: EMA smoothing factor (0.05–1.0) — lower = smoother, higher = more responsive

lifecycle.title: Schedule settings
lifecycle.description: Select how SmartShading detects night and morning. "Earliest trigger" fires as soon as either the configured time or the sun condition is reached — it does not combine or clamp them into a time window.

data_description.schedule_mode: "Same every day" uses one schedule for the whole week. "Weekday / Weekend" lets you set different times and positions for Mon–Fri versus Sat–Sun.

lifecycle_detail.title: Schedule details
lifecycle_detail.description: Enter the time or sun elevation for each trigger. Only the fields relevant to your selected trigger are shown.

data.night_sun_elevation: Custom night elevation (°) — only used when Custom is selected
data.morning_sun_elevation: Custom morning elevation (°) — only used when Custom is selected

data_description.night_position: Cover position during the night phase (0 = closed, 100 = open).
data_description.morning_position: Cover position at the start of the morning phase (0 = closed, 100 = open).
data_description.weekday_night_position: Cover position during the night phase on weekdays (0 = closed, 100 = open).
data_description.weekday_morning_position: Cover position at the start of the morning phase on weekdays (0 = closed, 100 = open).
data_description.weekend_night_position: Cover position during the night phase on weekends (0 = closed, 100 = open).
data_description.weekend_morning_position: Cover position at the start of the morning phase on weekends (0 = closed, 100 = open).
data_description.night_sun_event: Optional. If set, this astronomical event replaces the night time above. Dawn and dusk use civil twilight (sun at -6°).
data_description.morning_sun_event: Optional. If set, this astronomical event replaces the morning time above. Dawn and dusk use civil twilight (sun at -6°).

presence.description: Optional. If no one is selected, SmartShading always assumes someone is present and never triggers absence-based closing.

data_description.absence_position: Cover position while the zone is unoccupied, unless a stronger protection decision applies (0 = fully closed, 100 = fully open). Applies to all windows in this zone.
data_description.presence_policy: Controls how multiple presence entities are combined into one signal. Anyone home (default): present as soon as one selected person is home. Everyone home: present only when all selected people are home. Inverted: reverses the meaning of "anyone home" — present only once someone is confirmed away.

window.title: Add a window
window.description: Configure one window. SmartShading works with a precise azimuth — the compass direction is just a convenience shortcut.

data.floor_level: Floor level (-1 = basement, 0 = ground floor, 1 = 1st floor, …)
data.custom_azimuth: Custom azimuth (0-359°, only used if Orientation is set to Custom)

data_description.absence_position: Leave empty to inherit the zone's default absence position. Set a value to override it for this specific window only.
data_description.window_behavior_mode: Fully automatic: sun, comfort, absence, and schedule active. Absence and schedule: absence control, night, and morning schedule active; no solar, heat, or glare shading. Absence only: absence control only. Automation disabled: no automatic shading. Safety guards (storm/wind) always remain active.
data_description.floor_level: Used to derive the minimum sun elevation for direct exposure. −1 (basement) → 20°, 0 (ground floor) → 15°, 1 (1st floor) → 10°, 2 (2nd floor) → 5°, 3 and above → 0°. For windows in light wells or with heavy obstructions, use obstruction zones for more precise control.

cover_group.title: Covers for this window
cover_group.description: Select the cover entity or entities that shade the window "{window_name}". Only add covers that shade exactly this window surface. For separate windows, add separate windows.

add_another_window.title: Add another window?
add_another_window.description: {window_count} window(s) configured so far. You can finish now and use this zone with sensible defaults. Advanced per-window settings — sun sector, obstruction zones, window contacts, night ventilation and individual shade positions — are available later under Configure → Edit window.

comfort.title: Comfort settings
comfort.description: Configure shading protection features. Indoor temperature sensors are optional.

data_description.indoor_temperature_sensor_ids: One or more indoor sensors. When multiple sensors are configured, the average of all valid readings is used.
data_description.heat_protection_enabled: Prevents overheating by shading proactively based on sun position, weather, and temperatures.
data_description.glare_protection_enabled: Reduces direct sunlight when the sun hits the window.
data_description.solar_gain_enabled: Keeps shading more open in cool weather to support passive warming. Heat protection has priority.
data_description.glare_min_exposure_wm2: Glare protection only triggers when the window is in the sun's sector and the effective window exposure reaches at least this value. This prevents geometric sun position alone, without meaningful irradiance, from causing unnecessary shading.
data_description.heat_protection_hysteresis_c: How many degrees the temperature must drop below the heat protection threshold before shading is released again. Prevents rapid toggling near the threshold. Set to 0 to release exactly at the threshold (legacy behavior).

error.invalid_custom_azimuth: Enter a custom azimuth between 0 and 359 degrees, or pick a compass direction.
error.no_covers_selected: Select at least one cover entity for this window.
error.obstruction_elevation_range_invalid: "Blocked from elevation" must not be greater than "Blocked until elevation".
error.night_lift_requires_night_block: Option B (lift to ventilation position) requires Option A (block night move) to be enabled.
error.contact_sensor_required_for_block: Option A requires a contact sensor to be selected.
error.contact_sensor_required_for_lift: Option B requires a contact sensor to be selected.

error.morning_clamp_window_invalid: Morning earliest trigger must not be later than morning latest trigger.

create_entry.default: Zone created. You can adjust schedule, night behavior, and advanced options later from the zone settings using the gear icon.

options.bright: When it gets bright — nautical twilight (-12°)

options.lifecycle: At the end of the day/night cycle
options.first_comfort: On the next automatic comfort action
